description: Positioning a product by pairing sensory appeal (taste/flavor) with aggressive price positioning to create value perception and justify purchase decisions.
structural_recipe: Show or reference the product prominently. Lead with emotional descriptor of taste/quality (طعم + adjective). Immediately follow with price point in SAR, often preceded by 'بس' (only) to emphasize value. Use emoji reinforcement (fire, smiling face, food emoji). Structure as equation: [Quality] + [Low Price] = [Obvious Choice]. Include benefit statement or use case. Keep copy conversational and direct.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers are highly price-sensitive despite strong purchasing power. Pairing taste/quality claims with low price points addresses the cultural value of 'getting a deal' while maintaining aspirational flavor profiles. The 'بس' (only) messaging taps into bargain psychology. This reduces purchase friction by pre-answering the question 'is it worth it?' through juxtaposition rather than justification.
transplantation_caveats: Price point must be genuinely competitive in target market or messaging loses credibility
Risk of brand perception as 'cheap' if overused; requires balance with quality signaling
Currency-specific: messaging built around SAR prices may not translate directly to other markets
Assumes price-quality trade-off tolerance; luxury segments should avoid this framing
